article.body_preview:
Dhaka (AsiaNews) - Bangladesh is facing an energy crisis. Power cuts can last eight to 10 hours every day.

With the price of fuel to run generators rising and disruptions in energy supply in both cities and agricultural areas, doing business is a daily challenge.

For people like Bablu Ratno, a Catholic printer, outages mean higher costs and delays in delivering products to customers.

llm_connections.evidence_quote: Industry officials fear that disruptions in production will cut into exports, cause loss of foreign customers, as well as lead to large fines for non-compliance with contracts.
llm_connections.scope_justification: The article explicitly states production disruptions will 'cut into exports', directly addressing the exports component of the country's balance of trade as defined.
